title: Mail filters
creator: ag_leblond
content:
Hello,

I have defined about 45 simple filters to get rid of spam. They are all based on keywords found in subject or sender.

All of them are enabled and set this way :

Apply filter when : _ Manually Run X Getting New Mail ( Filter before Junk Classification ) _ Archiving _ After sending _ Periodically, every 10 minutes.

Perform these actions : 1. Set Junk Status to : Junk 2. Move Message to : "super-junk" folder 3. Mark As Read

See example in attached image.

PROBLEM : They do not run automatically when new emails are received. But if I run each of them manually, they work.

QUESTION : What is wrong ?

Thanks !

title: Can't Create Configuratio for mail.com
creator: ddmcleod
content:
For no obvious reason, Thunderbird started complaining about my existing configuration to mail.com. I tried changing passwords, I tried rebooting the computer, but nothing would work. I kept getting the "invalid password" popup when trying to connect. I've double-checked that mail.com is still working (I can access all my email from the web client no problem).

So, eventually, I deleted the account from Thunderbird altogether and now I'm trying to recreate the configuration. Thunderbird recognizes mail.com and correctly deduces the server configuration settings, but it will not connect. When I try to add the account I get this message:

"Authentication failed. Either the entered credentials are incorrect or a separate username is required for logging in. This username is usually your Windows domain login with or without the domain"

Why on earth would a "Windows domain login" be needed for a web-based email location?? When I had the configuration set up before, all I had to supply was my email address and password.

Something in Thunderbird has changed and I can't figure this out.

Meanwhile, I have about 10 other email accounts set up on Thunderbird and they all seem to be working properly.

Would sure be nice to find a solution so I can get my mail.com account back on Thunderbird.

Thanks for any help you can provide.

title: Thunderbird import not allowing to "match names" on import from a CSV file.
creator: ghbgiest
content:
Thunderbird import not allowing to "match names" on import from a CSV file.

Yes this is the problem when working with Thunderbird’s import of a CSV file, the Match field names option only is allowed on the first attempt. That told me that the source fields and first Record columns did not have useful fields to even try to Match field names. So I went and edited the CSV file to try to get at the Match Field names option but it was not available no matter what I fix or tried. So to me the problem is with Thunderbird’s “ surmising that since I was given the Match field Names screen that I Thunderbird never gives the Match Field names on subsequent attempt. Thunderbird needs to give the Match field Names screen as an option on every attempt not just the 1st attempt.

The below screen was given to me by one of the consultant’s attempt. And you can see that he had a CSV file with the THUNDERBIRD’s preferred CSV options!!! And it did use the Match Fields Option.

Attached screenshots is not possible at this time.

title: IMAP Delete/Expunge Failure on TB 140.7ESR with Gmail (macOS 15.2)
creator: jjg555
content: I am experiencing a persistent "ghosting" issue where deleted messages reappear in the "All Mail" folder. Environment: Mac Mini M2, macOS 15.2, Thunderbird 140.7ESR. Configuration: Gmail IMAP with "Auto-Expunge OFF" and "Immediately delete the message forever" selected in Gmail Web settings. Behavior: Using Shift+Delete or emptying the Trash in Thunderbird appears to work locally, but upon restart/re-index, messages return to the "All Mail" folder. Even after 2+ days, a search on the Gmail web interface still reveals the "deleted" items. Issue: The EXPUNGE command does not seem to be successfully committing to the Gmail server in this specific version build. Note that the TB has gone through some major rebuilds from 140.6 back to 128 ESR to 140.7ESR in the period from early Jan till 2 weeks ago. Very recently, TB became once again very slow on a start up, including after rebuilding of the large All Mail file of 66K items. A month ago, I added Satechi with 2TB drive split evenly between Time Machine and Other data. Significant oscillations took place in free memory on the MacHD between 60BG and 10 GB, currently at 58GB. Safari is my browser.

title: Defering or delaying compacting mailboxes
creator: palotta
content:
I would like to know how I can control the timing of compacting the mailboxes under Linux and Windows. The reason why I ask is that every time compacting needs to be done, the prompt to do so comes up as soon as the Thunderbird interface finishes loading but before all of the mailboxes are finished updating,which can take several minutes. If I accept the compacting, it inevitably returns an error that it could not run because the mailboxes are still updating. Or I can cancel until I go through this the next time.

It would seem like a simple matter to either give the user a way to delay the compacting process, or have Thunderbird not initiate it until it is safe to do so to begin with.

I have tried to research this but have not found any answers.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:148.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/148.0;tb_version:140.7.1esr (64-bit;os:Linux;kb_visits_prior:["/en-US/kb/thunderbird-import", "/en-US/kb/profiles-where-thunderbird-stores-user-data"]
